meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve Project Stalling
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on freelance projects. Master user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver polished, high-value experiences that clients love.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often 'feature creep' disguised as a lack of focus. By implementing a standardized, automated user onboarding flow, you force technical constraints early. This creates a clear finish line, reduces scope ambiguity, and ensures your clients see immediate value, effectively killing the project-stalling habit.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the initial spark of a new freelance project is electric, but three weeks in, the code feels like a labyrinth and the scope is bleeding into infinity. You are trapped in the '90% done' purgatory, watching your profit margins evaporate while your client grows impatient. You don't have a coding problem; you have a delivery problem.

To break this cycle, you must shift your architecture from 'building features' to 'guiding journeys.' By prioritizing user onboarding, you force yourself to define exactly what the user needs to succeed, trimming the fat before it ever hits the IDE. This isn't just about UI; it's about engineering a clear, repeatable path to project completion.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the singular action that proves value to your client's user. We will use this to anchor the entire onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core value proposition of this project and suggest a 3-step onboarding flow that drives the user to the 'Aha!' moment instantly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema for a user_onboarding_state table that tracks progress through the 3-step flow.'

steps.title: Architect the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Implement a state-driven approach so the UI always reflects where the user is in the onboarding journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React context provider and state machine logic to track user onboarding progress (onboarding_step: 0-3).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the OnboardingProvider.tsx file with state persistence to local storage.'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Walkthrough
steps.description: Create modular, reusable onboarding components that can be dropped into any project, saving you hours of boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular OnboardingStep component that accepts a title, description, and an action button, styled with Tailwind CSS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a reusable OnboardingStep component in components/ui/ and test its responsiveness.'

steps.title: Automate Success Triggers
steps.description: Ensure the application reacts when a user finishes the onboarding, triggering backend events or analytics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a hook useOnboardingComplete that triggers an API call to mark the user as 'onboarded' in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the useOnboardingComplete hook and connect it to our existing API service.'

steps.title: Finalize and Ship
steps.description: Use Claude Code to audit the flow, ensuring no edge cases exist where a user gets stuck.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the entire onboarding flow for potential UX blockers and suggest optimizations for speed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the onboarding flow for edge cases and suggest unit tests for the OnboardingProvider.'

pro_tips: Always build onboarding as a separate, pluggable module so you can copy-paste it into your next client project.
Use mock data for onboarding steps during development to verify flow before connecting to real database triggers.
Implement a 'Skip' button for power users; forcing onboarding is the fastest way to lose retention.
Use the state machine to track where users drop off, then use that data to justify 'scope adjustments' to your clients.
Keep the onboarding copy under 15 words per screen; clients pay for clarity, not complex documentation.
content.next_steps: After completing this, deploy your onboarding module as a private npm package or a boilerplate template to drastically reduce your setup time for future freelance contracts.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting incomplete projects drain your energy and reputation. By mastering the onboarding phase, you take control of the user experience and the project timeline. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
